2606.24644.title: Measuring User's Mental Models of Speech Translation in Human-AI Collaboration
2606.24644.paper_summary: Millions of people use machine translation (MT) tools daily, yet little is known about their perception of what systems can and cannot do. This paper studies users' mental models of speech translation systems through a new framework based on cross-lingual question answering, where users either accept MT output or request professional re-translation to answer questions based on the information presented in a foreign language. By analyzing user behavior and accuracy trends across varying translation qualities, we examine to what extent they can predict where the system is likely to be wrong, and how this mental model evolves. Users develop stronger mental models with practice, especially when they have some knowledge of the source language, primarily by relying on surface-level error cues. Moreover, providing speech transcriptions can help users develop better mental models. Our results show the promise of cross-lingual question answering as a downstream task for studying MT mental models and advancing our understanding of human-AI collaboration.
2606.24644.paper_summary_zh: 數百萬人每天使用機器翻譯（MT）工具，但對於他們對系統能做什麼和不能做什麼的認知卻知之甚少。本文通過一個基於跨語言問答的新框架研究用戶對語音翻譯系統的心理模型，該框架中用戶要麼接受MT輸出，要麼請求專業重新翻譯，以回答基於外語呈現的信息的問題。通過分析用戶行為和不同翻譯質量下的準確性趨勢，我們考察他們在多大程度上能預測系統可能出錯的地方，以及這種心理模型如何演變。用戶在練習中發展出更強的心理模型，尤其是在他們對源語言有一定了解的情況下，主要是依賴於表面錯誤提示。此外，提供語音轉錄可以幫助用戶發展更好的心理模型。我們的結果顯示，跨語言問答作為研究MT心理模型和推進我們對人類與AI合作理解的下游任務的潛力。
2606.24644.author: HyoJung Han et.al.
2606.24644.authors: HyoJung Han, Nishant Balepur, Jordan Boyd-Graber, Marine Carpuat
